shorts.title: State, local police begin 'Operation Nighthawk'
shorts.description: JESSUP, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU)— On Friday, 75 officers hit the roads of Northeastern Pennsylvania with one goal in mind: saving lives. 'Operation Nighthawk' is a massive DUI enforcement sweep taking place across Lackawanna County. 258 people died last year in Pennsylvania in crashes involving impaired drivers, but State Trooper Bob Urban says one is too [...]

shorts.title: Man found guilty of raping 18-year-old with autism
shorts.title_distilled: Man found guilty of raping 18-year-old with autism.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — A guilty verdict was issued for a Scranton man convicted of raping an intellectually disabled woman. A jury needed just 90 minutes to convict Mario Valentine following a four-day trial. The victim, an 18-year-old woman, was assaulted in 2024. Valentine faces up to 35 years in prison.
shorts.content: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — A guilty verdict was issued for a Scranton man convicted of raping an intellectually disabled woman. A jury needed just 90 minutes to convict Mario Valentine following a four-day trial. The victim, an 18-year-old woman, was assaulted in 2024. Valentine faces up to 35 years in prison.
shorts.content_distilled: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY  — A guilty verdict was issued for a Scranton man convicted of raping an intellectually disabled woman.

shorts.title: Local small businesses keep traditions alive in Scranton
shorts.title_distilled: Local small businesses keep traditions alive in Scranton.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — This week is National Small Business Week, and with Mother's Day coming up this weekend, local shops are seeing support pour in from their communities. 28/22 News visited a Scranton bakery to see how small businesses help keep local traditions alive. My Mother's Delicacies has been serving the community for [...]
